description: ClawHQ feature backlog — parallel tracks. See backlog/GAP-ANALYSIS.md for AS-IS/TO-BE analysis.

items.title: Cloud auth hooks (deferred)
items.description: OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (clawhq login), API key fallback (clawhq login --token), agent registration (clawhq register), cloud status/disconnect. Storage: ~/.clawhq/auth.json (0600 perms). DEFERRED — only build when Tier 3 cloud service is being developed. Until then these would be dead code.
items.groomingNotes: DEFERRED — correct. Cloud auth is dead code until Tier 3 service exists. Don't build the client before the server exists.

items.title: Standalone binary distribution (deferred)
items.description: Node.js SEA with ESM + esbuild bundle → SEA blob → postject injection. 4-platform matrix (linux/darwin × x64/arm64). curl installer script (scripts/install.sh). Checksums in GitHub Release. DEFERRED — ship npm-only first, validate SEA compatibility later.
items.groomingNotes: DEFERRED — npm global install covers the target audience (they have Node.js). SEA binary is a distribution optimization not a user value story. Revisit after npm package is proven.

items.title: "Replace my X" migration wizard — clawhq migrate --replace
items.description: Guided flow for replacing a specific big-tech product end-to-end. clawhq migrate --replace 'google assistant' (or 'chatgpt', 'apple intelligence') maps the product's features to OpenClaw equivalents, identifies gaps, sets up integrations, imports routines/history, and produces a comparison checklist (checkmark = covered, warning = workaround, X = not supported). Depends on FEAT-102 and FEAT-103. This is the positioning story: makes switching feel like an upgrade rather than a sacrifice. PRODUCT.md Phase 3 item.
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: FEAT-102 (ChatGPT import) and FEAT-103 (Google Assistant import) are both done. This is the positioning feature — 'Replace my Google Assistant' as a guided experience. Sprint scope: (1) Create product-feature mapping for 3 products (Google Assistant, ChatGPT, Apple Intelligence) — what features each has, which are covered by OpenClaw, which have workarounds, which are gaps; (2) clawhq migrate --replace 'google assistant' runs the appropriate import (FEAT-102/103), sets up matching blueprint, shows comparison checklist; (3) Output: checkmark/warning/X table per feature. Acceptance criteria: at least 3 products mapped; checklist renders honestly (gaps marked as X not hidden); tsc clean.

items.title: Email Manager blueprint YAML + init integration
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/email-manager.yaml with full operational profile for 'Run my emails' use case. Identity: professional, concise, protective of time. Tools: email + calendar + tasks. Skills: email-digest, morning-brief. Cron: inbox check 15min, digest 8am. Security: hardened, egress mail server only. Wire into blueprint loader so `clawhq init --guided` offers it as a choice. This proves the use-case blueprint model end-to-end with existing code.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: This proves the use-case blueprint model. The 6 existing generic templates in configs/templates/ work. This adds the first domain-specific YAML. Sprint scope: (1) Create configs/templates/email-manager.yaml following the existing YAML schema (see configs/templates/ for examples); (2) Define identity, tools (email + calendar + tasks), skills (email-digest, morning-brief), cron schedules, security posture (hardened, egress mail only), autonomy level; (3) Verify it loads via getBuiltInTemplates() and appears in clawhq init --guided and clawhq template list; (4) No code changes needed — just the YAML file following existing schema. Acceptance criteria: template appears in list, init wizard offers it, generates valid config.

items.title: Stock Trading Assistant blueprint YAML
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/stock-trading.yaml. Identity: analytical, data-driven, conservative risk warnings. Tools: quote, web-search, tasks. Skills: market-scan, portfolio-alert. Cron: pre-market 6am, hourly during market. Security: hardened, egress finance APIs + email only. Autonomy: auto-monitor, flag before trade suggestions.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-106 — YAML file following existing schema in configs/templates/. No code changes. Can sprint in parallel with other blueprint YAMLs.

items.title: Meal Planner blueprint YAML
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/meal-planner.yaml. Identity: health-conscious, practical. Tools: web-search, tasks, calendar. Skills: meal-plan, shopping-list. Cron: weekly plan Sunday 6pm, daily prep. Autonomy: auto-plan within dietary prefs, approval for shopping list.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-106 — YAML file following existing schema in configs/templates/. No code changes. Can sprint in parallel with other blueprint YAMLs.

items.title: Founder's Ops blueprint YAML
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/founders-ops.yaml. Identity: professional, decisive, protective of time. Tools: email, calendar, tasks, web-search. Skills: email-digest, investor-update, hiring-pipeline. Cron: morning triage 7am, weekly investor prep. Autonomy: auto-triage, approval for external sends.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-106 — YAML file following existing schema in configs/templates/. No code changes. Can sprint in parallel with other blueprint YAMLs.

items.title: Family Hub blueprint YAML
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/family-hub.yaml. Identity: warm, organized, patient. Tools: calendar, tasks, web-search. Skills: meal-plan, chore-assign, family-brief. Cron: daily brief, weekly meal plan, chore rotation. Autonomy: auto-schedule, approval for external messages.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-106 — YAML file following existing schema in configs/templates/. No code changes. Can sprint in parallel with other blueprint YAMLs.

items.title: AI Blog Maintainer blueprint YAML
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/ai-blog.yaml. Identity: editorial voice, thoughtful, original perspectives. Tools: web-search, tasks. Skills: research-digest, draft-post. Cron: daily research, weekly draft. Autonomy: auto-research, approval before publishing.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-106 — YAML file following existing schema in configs/templates/. No code changes. Can sprint in parallel with other blueprint YAMLs.

items.title: Blueprint personalizer: preference questions per blueprint
items.description: Extend init wizard to ask blueprint-specific preference questions. Email Manager: communication style, priority contacts, auto-reply policy. Trading: risk tolerance, markets, alert threshold. Meal: dietary restrictions, cuisine preferences, household size. Apply answers to customize blueprint before generating config. 1-3 extra questions per blueprint, not a long form.
items.groomingNotes: UNBLOCKED: Blueprint YAMLs (FEAT-106-111) provide the blueprints to personalize. Sprint scope: (1) Add optional 'customization_questions' array to blueprint YAML schema — each question has id, prompt, type (text/select/boolean), default, applies_to (which config field it modifies); (2) Extend init wizard to ask these questions after blueprint selection; (3) Apply answers to customize generated config before writing. Keep it to 1-3 questions per blueprint. Acceptance criteria: Email Manager asks about communication style and priority contacts; answers reflected in generated config; blueprints without questions work unchanged.

items.title: clawhq install: pre-req detection + deployment scaffold
items.description: Single command: detect Docker (guide if missing), detect Node.js >=20 (guide if missing), detect Ollama (optional), check disk space. Create ~/.clawhq/ directory with clawhq.yaml meta-config. Scaffold engine/, workspace/, ops/, security/, cron/, cloud/ subdirs. This is the foundation — no engine acquisition yet, just the structure and checks.
items.groomingNotes: P0 — THE ENTRY POINT. Without this, ClawHQ isn't installable as a product. Sprint scope: (1) Create src/build/installer/detect.ts — check Docker (docker --version), Node.js >=20 (node --version), Ollama (ollama --version, optional), disk space (>2GB); (2) Create src/build/installer/scaffold.ts — mkdir -p ~/.clawhq/{engine,workspace,ops,security,cron,cloud}, write clawhq.yaml with version + install date + trust_mode: paranoid; (3) Create src/cli/install.ts — createInstallCommand() that runs detection then scaffolding, with friendly guidance for missing prereqs; (4) Register in cli/index.ts. Acceptance criteria: clawhq install on fresh system detects prereqs, creates ~/.clawhq/ structure, writes clawhq.yaml; missing Docker shows install guidance; tsc clean.

items.title: clawhq install: engine acquisition from source
items.description: Extend clawhq install to clone OpenClaw source, run existing two-stage Docker build (src/docker/build.ts already works), store in ~/.clawhq/engine/. Wire existing src/source/ module. --from-source flag. Default path for now — trusted cache comes later when we have a release server.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED on FEAT-113 (scaffold). Sprint scope: extend clawhq install to clone OpenClaw repo into ~/.clawhq/engine/, run existing two-stage Docker build. Wire existing src/build/source/ module. --from-source flag. Acceptance criteria: clawhq install --from-source clones, builds, stores engine in ~/.clawhq/engine/.

items.title: Migrate file paths from ~/.openclaw/ to ~/.clawhq/engine/
items.description: Update all hardcoded ~/.openclaw/ references to use configurable deployment root from clawhq.yaml. Default: ~/.clawhq/engine/ for new installs, ~/.openclaw/ for existing. Add migration command: clawhq migrate-layout that moves files from old to new structure. Non-breaking — existing deployments keep working.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED on FEAT-113 (scaffold). HIGH IMPACT — every hardcoded ~/.openclaw/ path in the codebase must become configurable. Sprint scope: (1) grep for ~/.openclaw/ across src/, extract all path references; (2) Create config/paths.ts with deploymentRoot() that reads from clawhq.yaml or defaults to ~/.openclaw/ for backward compat; (3) Replace all hardcoded paths; (4) clawhq migrate-layout moves files from ~/.openclaw/ to ~/.clawhq/engine/. Non-breaking for existing installs.

items.title: Skill: email-digest (triage and summarize inbox)
items.description: Workspace skill that triages inbox: categorize emails (urgent/action/info/spam), summarize key messages, propose responses for approval. SKILL.md + supporting prompts. Used by Email Manager and Founder's Ops blueprints. Leverages existing email tool wrapper.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Skill infrastructure (FEAT-088, FEAT-089) is done. This is the first real skill that proves the model. Sprint scope: (1) Create workspace skill YAML + prompts in configs/skills/email-digest/; (2) SKILL.md with triage categories (urgent/action/info/spam), summary format, proposed response template; (3) Cron integration — runs on inbox check schedule from blueprint; (4) Uses existing email tool wrapper. Acceptance criteria: clawhq skill install email-digest works from built-in registry; skill triages and summarizes inbox; output is useful for Email Manager blueprint.

items.title: Skill: auto-reply (draft + approval gate)
items.description: Workspace skill that drafts email replies matching user's voice. Queues via existing approval system (src/approval/) before sending. Learns from corrections. SKILL.md + prompts. Used by Email Manager blueprint.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-116. Skill YAML + prompts + SKILL.md. Each is independent and can sprint in parallel.

items.title: Skill: market-scan (pre-market research digest)
items.description: Workspace skill for daily market scan. Uses quote tool for watchlist prices, web-search for overnight news, generates morning brief with key moves and alerts. SKILL.md + prompts. Used by Stock Trading blueprint.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-116. Skill YAML + prompts + SKILL.md. Each is independent and can sprint in parallel.

items.title: Skill: meal-plan (weekly planning + shopping list)
items.description: Workspace skill that generates weekly meal plans based on dietary preferences, generates shopping lists, schedules prep reminders via calendar. SKILL.md + prompts. Used by Meal Planner and Family Hub blueprints.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Same pattern as FEAT-116. Skill YAML + prompts + SKILL.md. Each is independent and can sprint in parallel.

items.title: Trust mode config + kill switch
items.description: Add trust_mode field to clawhq.yaml: paranoid (default, no cloud), zero-trust, managed. CLI: clawhq cloud mode [paranoid|zero-trust|managed]. Kill switch: clawhq cloud disconnect — sets to paranoid immediately. Foundation for all cloud features. No cloud server needed yet — just the local config and mode switching.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Foundation for all cloud features. No server needed. Sprint scope: (1) Add trust_mode field to clawhq.yaml schema (paranoid/zero-trust/managed, default paranoid); (2) clawhq cloud mode [mode] CLI command to read/set; (3) clawhq cloud disconnect sets to paranoid immediately (kill switch); (4) Mode stored in ~/.clawhq/clawhq.yaml. Acceptance criteria: clawhq cloud mode shows current mode; switching modes works; disconnect is instant.

items.title: Health heartbeat: outbound-only status reporter
items.description: Agent-initiated HTTPS POST to configurable endpoint. Reports: agent state, uptime, container health, integration status, memory sizes, version, error codes. NEVER reports content. Runs on interval from clawhq.yaml. Logs every outbound payload to ops/audit/cloud.jsonl for user inspection. clawhq cloud audit shows what was sent.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED on FEAT-120 (trust mode config must exist first). Outbound-only health reporter — no server needed to build the client side.

items.title: Command queue: pull, verify signature, execute or reject
items.description: Agent polls cloud endpoint for pending commands. Each command is cryptographically signed. Agent verifies against pinned public key, checks command against allowed list for current trust mode, logs to audit trail, executes or rejects. Content-access commands have NO handler (architecturally blocked). Allowed ops commands: restart, check-update, trigger-backup.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED on FEAT-120. Command queue client — can build against mock endpoint initially.

items.title: agentd: managed mode daemon wrapper
items.description: Wrapper that runs ClawHQ platform as a daemon. Starts heartbeat (FEAT-121), starts command poller (FEAT-122), manages lifecycle. Installable as systemd/launchd service. Same platform, automated lifecycle. Foundation for managed hosting.

items.title: credentials.json separate credential store
items.description: Add credentials.json (mode 0600) alongside .env for integration credentials. CRUD via clawhq creds add/remove/list (extend existing creds command). Health probe integration reads from credentials.json. Migration: existing .env credentials auto-copied on first run. Non-breaking.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: GAP 5 from gap analysis. Sprint scope: (1) Create credentials.json store alongside .env; (2) Extend existing clawhq creds command with add/remove/list; (3) Health probe reads from credentials.json; (4) Auto-migrate existing .env credentials on first run. Non-breaking.

items.title: Monitor daemon with configurable alerts
items.description: Background health check loop using existing alerts/ system. ops/monitor/monitor.yaml defines what to watch + thresholds. ops/monitor/alerts.yaml routes alerts to terminal, webhook, or cloud. clawhq monitor start/stop/status. Wraps existing predictive health (src/alerts/) into a daemon.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Wraps existing alerts/ system into a background daemon. Sprint scope: (1) monitor.yaml config for check intervals and thresholds; (2) clawhq monitor start/stop/status; (3) Routes alerts via notification dispatcher (FEAT-081, done). Runs as background process, not systemd (that's for agentd).

items.title: Managed hosting: DigitalOcean provisioning
items.description: clawhq cloud provision --provider digitalocean. Create droplet, install platform via cloud-init, configure agentd, setup DNS + SSL (Let's Encrypt). Requires agentd (FEAT-123) and cloud console to exist.

items.title: Cloud console web app MVP
items.description: Separate web application for clawhq.com. Account management, agent health dashboard from heartbeats, fleet view. Never shows agent content. Separate repo.

items.title: Blueprint marketplace: submission + search
items.description: Community blueprint contributions. YAML submission format, validation (can't loosen Layer 1 security), clawhq blueprint submit/search. Requires cloud console.

items.title: Trusted cache engine acquisition (default install path)
items.description: Default clawhq install path: download pre-built OpenClaw engine from clawhq.com with SHA256 + GPG signature verification. Faster than --from-source. Requires release infrastructure to publish signed artifacts. This is the 'Privacy Migrant' install path — no git clone, no build, just verify and run.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED ON EXTERNAL INFRA: Requires clawhq.com release server publishing signed artifacts — cannot be sprint-ready until that exists. FEAT-114 (from-source) is the actual install path in the meantime. Downgrade to should — this is the polish story once infrastructure is ready, not blocking launch.

items.title: Paranoid posture: encrypted workspace + integrity hashing
items.description: Enhance Paranoid security posture per ARCHITECTURE.md spec. Three additions: (1) Encrypted workspace volume using LUKS or fscrypt, (2) Identity file integrity hashing — hash SOUL.md/AGENTS.md at deploy, verify at runtime, alert on tampering, (3) Stricter resource limits per posture (4CPU/4GB standard, 2/2GB hardened, 1/1GB paranoid). Currently only standard/hardened container hardening is differentiated.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Container hardening infrastructure exists in src/build/docker/hardening.ts. Sprint scope: (1) Add resource limit differentiation to docker-compose generation — read posture from config, apply CPU/RAM limits per ARCHITECTURE.md table; (2) Add identity integrity hashing — hash SOUL.md + AGENTS.md at build time, store hashes in ~/.clawhq/security/identity-hashes.json, add doctor check that verifies hashes at runtime; (3) Encrypted workspace is complex (LUKS requires privileges) — scope as a separate follow-up or use Docker volume encryption. Start with resource limits + integrity hashing. Acceptance criteria: docker-compose.yml has different CPU/RAM limits per posture; clawhq doctor verifies identity file integrity in paranoid mode; tsc clean.

items.title: Security posture.yaml file + management
items.description: Create and manage ~/.clawhq/security/posture.yaml as specified in ARCHITECTURE.md deployment directory structure. Currently security posture is a config field in docker-compose generation but no standalone file exists. This file should be the source of truth for posture (standard/hardened/paranoid), read by hardening, doctor, and status modules. clawhq secure posture [standard|hardened|paranoid] to change.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Sprint scope: (1) Create posture.yaml schema — posture level, last changed date, overrides; (2) Write posture.yaml during init/install to ~/.clawhq/security/; (3) Read from posture.yaml in hardening.ts instead of (or in addition to) config field; (4) Add clawhq secure posture [level] CLI subcommand; (5) Doctor check verifies posture.yaml matches applied container config. Acceptance criteria: posture.yaml created on init; clawhq secure posture shows current; changing posture triggers rebuild prompt; tsc clean.

items.title: PII scan in deploy preflight
items.description: Wire PII + secrets scanner into deploy preflight checks. PRODUCT.md specifies scanning before deploy to catch leaked secrets in workspace files, identity files, and config. The scanner exists (src/secure/secrets/scanner.ts) and the preflight exists (src/build/launcher/preflight.ts) — they just need connecting. Fail preflight if high-severity secrets found.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Both modules exist, just need wiring. Sprint scope: (1) Add PII/secrets scan step to preflight.ts — call scanFiles() from secure/secrets/scanner.ts on workspace/ and identity files; (2) Fail preflight if high-severity matches found (API keys, passwords in plaintext); (3) Warn on medium-severity (email addresses, phone numbers in non-identity files); (4) --force flag to override. Acceptance criteria: clawhq up fails if .env secrets leaked into workspace files; warning on PII in unexpected locations; --force bypasses; tsc clean.

items.title: Cloud audit trail — clawhq cloud audit
items.description: Log every outbound payload sent to cloud (heartbeat, command responses) to ~/.clawhq/ops/audit/cloud.jsonl. clawhq cloud audit shows what was sent, when, and to where. ARCHITECTURE.md promises full transparency — users can inspect every byte that left their machine toward clawhq.com.
items.groomingNotes: BLOCKED on FEAT-121 (heartbeat — nothing to audit until outbound payloads exist). Sprint scope when unblocked: (1) Create cloud audit appender that logs every outbound HTTP payload to cloud.jsonl — timestamp, endpoint, payload (full), response status; (2) Wire into heartbeat sender and command queue responder; (3) clawhq cloud audit CLI command to display entries. Acceptance criteria: every outbound cloud payload logged; clawhq cloud audit shows full history; --since flag for filtering.

items.title: Agent growth metrics — 30/60/90 day tracking
items.description: Track and report agent capability growth over time. PRODUCT.md promises 'measurable increase in autonomous task completion at 30/60/90 days'. Metrics: skills installed, integrations active, tasks completed autonomously, approval rate, tool usage frequency, memory tier sizes. clawhq status --metrics shows growth trajectory. Weekly snapshot stored in ~/.clawhq/ops/metrics/.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: All data sources exist (skills registry, approval queue, evolve history, memory store). Sprint scope: (1) Create metrics collector that snapshots key counts weekly — skills count, integrations count, approval accept/reject ratio, memory tier sizes, evolve history length; (2) Store in ~/.clawhq/ops/metrics/weekly.jsonl; (3) Add --metrics flag to clawhq status that shows growth chart (text-based sparklines or simple table comparing week-over-week); (4) Doctor check: warn if no growth in 30 days (agent may be staling). Acceptance criteria: weekly snapshots collected; clawhq status --metrics shows trajectory; 30-day stale warning in doctor.

items.title: Align SOUL.md references — docs vs code
items.description: README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md reference SOUL.md as a blueprint-generated identity file. Code generates IDENTITY.md instead. 19 source files reference SOUL.md (smoke checks, file permissions, governance, identity generators, etc.). Decide canonical name and align everywhere. If SOUL.md is the brand name, rename IDENTITY.md generator output. If IDENTITY.md is correct, update docs.
items.groomingNotes: READY TO SPRINT: Naming inconsistency between docs and code. 19 source files reference SOUL.md. Sprint scope: (1) Audit all 19 files to determine whether they check for SOUL.md existence, generate it, or validate it; (2) Decision: SOUL.md is the more evocative brand name (README uses it prominently) — recommend renaming IDENTITY.md output to SOUL.md; (3) Update identity generator in src/design/identity/identity.ts to output SOUL.md; (4) Update all references in smoke checks, doctor checks, file permissions, governance; (5) Update docs if going the other direction. Acceptance criteria: one canonical name used everywhere — docs, code, generated files; no references to the old name; tsc clean; tests pass.

items.title: Replace Google Assistant blueprint YAML
items.description: Create configs/blueprints/replace-google-assistant.yaml — full-day orchestration blueprint. Identity: proactive, organised, low-friction. Tools: email + calendar + tasks + web-search. Skills: morning-brief, email-digest, schedule-guard. Cron: morning brief 7am, inbox check 15min, heartbeat 10min. Security: hardened. Autonomy: high for scheduling/triage, approval gate for replies and web searches. Rounds out the core blueprint set from PRODUCT.md alongside Email Manager, Stock Trading, Meal Planner, Blog, Founders Ops, Family Hub.
items.groomingNotes: GAP: Listed in PRODUCT.md blueprint set but missing from backlog. Completes the 7-blueprint core library. UNBLOCKED once FEAT-106 (Email Manager) is done — same schema, same wiring. Sprint scope: (1) Create configs/blueprints/replace-google-assistant.yaml; (2) Identity: proactive, organised, anticipates needs; (3) Tools: email + calendar + tasks + web-search; (4) Skills: morning-brief, email-digest, schedule-guard; (5) Cron: 7am morning brief, 15min inbox check, 10min heartbeat; (6) Wire into blueprint loader. Acceptance criteria: blueprint appears in clawhq blueprint list; init wizard offers it; generates valid config.

items.title: Skill: morning-brief (daily proactive summary)
items.description: Workspace skill that generates a daily morning brief: upcoming calendar events, prioritised email summary, active tasks, weather if configured, focus time recommendations. SKILL.md + prompts. Used by Email Manager, Replace Google Assistant, and Founder's Ops blueprints. Runs via cron (typically 7-8am). Output sent to user's messaging channel (Signal/Telegram/Discord).
items.groomingNotes: GAP: Referenced in Email Manager (FEAT-106), Replace Google Assistant (FEAT-139), and Founder's Ops (FEAT-109) blueprints but missing from backlog. Skill infrastructure done (FEAT-088, FEAT-089). Sprint scope: (1) Create configs/skills/morning-brief/ with SKILL.md + prompts; (2) SKILL.md defines: calendar query (today's events), email triage summary (top 5 urgent), task list snapshot, formatted morning message; (3) Cron-triggered — runs at user-configured time (default 7am); (4) Uses existing email + calendar + tasks tool wrappers; (5) clawhq skill install morning-brief from built-in registry. Acceptance criteria: morning-brief skill installs cleanly; generates useful daily brief; output reaches user's channel.

items.title: Skill: research-digest (web research summary)
items.description: Workspace skill that runs web searches on configured topics and produces a structured digest: key findings, source links, relevance score. Used by Stock Trading Assistant (market news), AI Blog Maintainer (topic research), and any blueprint needing regular research summaries. Configurable topics, search depth, and digest format per blueprint.
items.groomingNotes: GAP: Referenced in Stock Trading Assistant (FEAT-107) and AI Blog Maintainer (FEAT-111) blueprints but missing from backlog. Sprint scope: (1) Create configs/skills/research-digest/ with SKILL.md + prompts; (2) SKILL.md defines: topic list (from blueprint config), web-search tool calls, deduplication, structured output (headline + summary + source + relevance); (3) Blueprint-configurable topic list and digest format; (4) Uses existing web-search tool wrapper; (5) Cron-triggered per blueprint (e.g. pre-market 6am for trading, daily for blog). Acceptance criteria: configurable topics produce useful research summaries; works with market-scan and draft-post skills downstream.

_dependsOn_docs: Items may include an optional 'dependsOn' array of item ID strings (e.g. "dependsOn": ["FEAT-001"]). Auto-pick skips items whose dependencies are not yet done.
_phaseMapping: Archived — original phase mapping for FEAT-001 through FEAT-047 (all done, items removed from active backlog)
